name: planning
summary: Decompose a complex goal into a dependency-ordered plan, then execute. Unlike prompt-chaining (which has fixed phases), planning produces the plan at runtime.
when_to_use: Multi-day or multi-component work where the breakdown itself is part of the intelligence needed.

layers.CLAUDE.md: Add rule: 'For work over 1 hour: write a plan first. Plan includes constraints, phases, exit criteria per phase, rollback.'
layers.skill: skills/deep-code-intelligence enforces plan-first for non-trivial work.
layers.hook: Optional: PreToolUse hook that blocks Write operations until .claude/active-task.md exists (same pattern as autonomy-planner).
layers.agent: agents/autonomy-planner writes the plan to .claude/active-task.md. agents/principal-engineer-strategist is the higher-reasoning variant.

cost: ~1.2× (one extra upfront pass). Usually pays back 5-10× by avoiding wasted work.
anti_patterns: Plans that don't include exit criteria per phase — you can't tell when a phase is done.
Plans without rollback — recovery becomes guesswork when something breaks.
Writing a plan then ignoring it — plan-as-theater.
